title: Machine Learning for Subseasonal-to-Seasonal Forecasting
description: Developing deep learning architectures that can skillfully predict climate variability on the 2–6 week timescale, where useful signal still exists in the ocean and sea-ice state.

title: Mechanistic Interpretability Applied to Climate Models
description: Borrowing sparse autoencoder techniques from LLM interpretability to understand what representations physics-informed neural networks learn from atmospheric data.

title: Causal Discovery in Climate Time Series
description: Applying constraint-based and score-based causal discovery to multivariate climate datasets to identify robust causal relationships between climate indices.
